benefits.description: Significantly reduced pain, able to undergo ganglion impar block injection (several inches deep into bone) with no anesthesia or sedation
Reduction of surgical scarring in pelvic area that had not improved in 2 years prior to BPC-157 use
Reduced pain associated with endometriosis lesions and internal scar tissue
Dramatically less anesthesia required during medical procedures

protocol_notes: 5mg BPC-157 dissolved in approximately 3 CCs of BAC water. Taking approximately 1/15th to 1/10th of the solution per dose, equating to roughly 500-1000 mcg per dose. Injecting subcutaneously in areas surrounding the abdomen near the site of most pain, and also taking orally to increase general systemic concentrations. Using both routes simultaneously. Doctors informed and supportive. Has medical assistant certification. Dose described as pulling back to 20-30 tick mark on a 1mL syringe.
